perTest.details: PASSED (pytest: 1 passed in 1.79s). Repeatable-650 non-destructive update: created a throwaway seed bib with three 650 subject headings, ran default replace_first update_marc_field(mms,'650',{'a':'Physics -- data reduction'}); re-fetch showed all three 650s survive (len==3), first occurrence rewritten in place, Astrophysics + Cosmology preserved verbatim. Seed bib deleted (CLEANUP OK). Live corroboration of #184 3->1 silent-data-loss fix; offline pin tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_184.py.

PASSED (pytest: 1 passed in 1.82s). mode='append' path: created a three-650 seed bib, ran update_marc_field(mms,'650',{'a':'Particle physics'},mode='append'); re-fetch showed four 650s (len==4) with the three originals intact plus the appended heading, no deletion. Seed bib deleted (CLEANUP OK). Covers the #184 supported-non-destructive-append acceptance criterion.

PASSED (pytest: 1 passed in 1.89s). Ordered [code,value]-pair editing path with repeated $x: created a single-650 seed bib, ran update_marc_field(mms,'650',[['a','Science'],['x','History'],['x','20th century']]); re-fetch round-tripped pairs_650 == [('a','Science'),('x','History'),('x','20th century')] with both distinct $x values in order (impossible via the pre-fix dict path). Seed bib deleted (CLEANUP OK). Covers #185 dict-typed-subfield fix; offline pin test_issue_185.py.

PASSED (pytest: 1 passed in 2.68s). Escaping round-trip: created a 245 seed bib, ran update_marc_field(mms,'245',[['a','Law & order <suppl> report']],ind1='1',ind2='0'); re-fetch decoded 245 $a exactly once to the literal 'Law & order <suppl> report' and raw MARCXML carried no doubly-escaped '&amp;'. Seed bib deleted (CLEANUP OK). Covers #186 double-escaping fix; offline pin test_issue_186.py.
